title: Wire Cutting and Insulation Stripping — Blade Mechanics
content: Wire stripping uses two opposing V-notch blades that penetrate insulation to controlled depth without cutting conductor. Blade gap g = conductor_diameter + 0.1 mm clearance. Cutting force F = σ_UTS × A_insulation / (efficiency factor 0.6–0.8).
extended_content: Automatic wire stripper sequence: 1) Feed — servo/stepper advances wire by strip length L (programmable 5–50 mm). 2) Clamp — jaws hold wire. 3) Cut — blades close to set depth (adjusted by cam stop). 4) Pull — blades retract while jaws hold wire, stripping insulation. 5) Release. Blade material: hardened tool steel HRC 58–62. Wire sizes: AWG 20–26 (0.5–1.5 mm²). Conductor damage detection: inline resistance check post-strip. Solenoid-actuated or servo-actuated blade drive. Throughput: 300–600 wires/hour automated. Wire cutter (full cut): shear blade F_cut = π × d_wire² / 4 × σ_UTS(copper) = 210 MPa × area.
formula: F_strip ≈ σ_UTS · A_insul · L_contact / η ;  blade_gap = d_conductor + 0.1 mm
units: F: N, σ: MPa, A: mm², L: mm, d: mm
source: IPC/WHMA-A-620 Wire Harness Standard; Panduit Wire Processing Guide; Schleuniger Application Notes
